notes: Patterns ported from the original Cashiro TigoPesaParser.kt (parser-core/src/main/kotlin/com/ritesh/parser/core/bank/TigoPesaParser.kt). Tigo Pesa / Mixx by Yas is a Tanzanian mobile-money wallet, so like JioPay there is no real bank account number in the SMS — transactions fall back to the "0000" wallet account so resolution can match a linked wallet. Faithfulness notes vs the Kotlin parser: - Kotlin's isTransactionMessage requires "tsh" AND one of the transaction keywords; the engine filter only supports OR, so the "tsh" requirement is dropped (a keyword-bearing message without TSh would fail amount extraction and surface as REVIEW instead of REJECTED). - The agent pattern in Kotlin captures only the name and re-prefixes "Agent - "; here the extractor captures the "Agent - NAME" span verbatim, which yields the same value for the canonical "Agent - NAME" format. - The TIPS source mapping (Selcom/NMB/CRDB -> friendly names) is expressed as pipeline setFieldWhen steps keyed on the whole body instead of just the captured TIPS source token.
